name: Mike Abene
abstract: Michael Abene (born July 2, 1942) is an American jazz pianist known for accompanying singers and arranging music.

interviews.title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

concerts.label: New York City Gay Men's Chorus

about_quotes.speaker: Karolina Strassmayer
about_quotes.evidence: he is such a hero to me. He’s maybe ten years or eleven years that he was our principal conductor.

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: I got Mike Abene, Joe Rocisano, Pete Robinson, Chris Gunning, and a couple of more, Gary Anderson. All young, kind of cutting edge writers, with a huge orchestra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Lanny Morgan
about_quotes.evidence: I had a little group with Mike Abene and Ronnie McClure... had a little quintet
about_quotes.doc_title: Lanny Morgan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: George Handy
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Mike was playing up there a while ago, Mike Abene. [INTERVIEWEE] Right. They have ads on their front windows about all the jazz and artists they have.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: I spoke to Mike Abene and Vince Mendoza and Chuck Owen... I know Mike Abene wrote in some extensions in the tunes
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007
